Footer.tagline: All tools run in your browser. No files are ever uploaded.

Home.badge: No uploads. No accounts. No tracking.
Home.headline: Tools that work
Home.subheadline: Every tool runs entirely in your browser. Your files never leave your device.
Home.privacyTitle: Your files stay on your device
Home.privacyBody: FileFolks uses WebAssembly and browser APIs to process files locally. Nothing is ever sent to a server. You can verify this by checking the Network tab in your browser's developer tools.

pdf-merge.name: Merge PDF
pdf-merge.description: Combine multiple PDF files into one document
pdf-merge.longDescription: Drag and drop your PDF files to merge them into a single document. Reorder pages by dragging. Everything happens in your browser — your files never leave your device.
pdf-merge.seoTitle: Merge PDF Files Online — Free, Private, No Upload | FileFolks
pdf-merge.seoDescription: Combine multiple PDFs into one file. Runs in your browser with no upload. Drag, drop, reorder, and download.

faqs.question: Are my PDF files uploaded to a server?
faqs.answer: No. All merging happens locally in your browser using the pdf-lib library. Your files never leave your device.

faqs.question: Is there a limit on the number of PDFs I can merge?
faqs.answer: There is no fixed limit. The practical limit depends on your device's available memory. Most users can merge 50+ files without issues.

faqs.question: Can I reorder pages before merging?
faqs.answer: Yes. After dropping your files, you can drag them into the order you want. The merged PDF will follow this order.

faqs.question: Will the merged PDF keep bookmarks and links?
faqs.answer: Basic page content, images, and text are preserved. Interactive elements like bookmarks and form fields may not carry over in all cases.

compress-pdf.name: Compress PDF
compress-pdf.description: Reduce PDF file size while preserving content
compress-pdf.longDescription: Drop a PDF to reduce its file size. The tool strips embedded metadata and optimizes the internal structure entirely in your browser. Your file is never uploaded.
compress-pdf.seoTitle: Compress PDF Online — Free, Private, No Upload | FileFolks
compress-pdf.seoDescription: Reduce PDF file size in your browser. No upload needed. Strips metadata and optimizes internal structure for smaller files.

faqs.question: Is my PDF uploaded to a server?
faqs.answer: No. All compression happens in your browser using the pdf-lib library. Your file never leaves your device. You can confirm this by watching your browser's Network tab while using the tool.

faqs.question: How much will my PDF shrink?
faqs.answer: The tool strips embedded metadata and optimizes the internal object structure. Savings vary by file: PDFs with redundant metadata or an unoptimized internal layout can see 10-40% reduction. PDFs that are already optimized or consist mostly of compressed images will see smaller gains.

faqs.question: Will the content of my PDF change?
faqs.answer: No. Only invisible metadata (author, creator, timestamps) and internal structural overhead are removed. Page content, text, images, and layout are preserved exactly.

faqs.question: What if the compressed file is larger than the original?
faqs.answer: This can happen if the original PDF was already tightly optimized. The tool will still let you download both versions, but we recommend keeping your original in that case.

json-formatter.name: JSON Formatter
json-formatter.description: Format, validate, and minify JSON data
json-formatter.longDescription: Paste or drop a JSON file to format, validate, and inspect it. See key counts, nesting depth, and file size at a glance. Everything runs in your browser — nothing is uploaded.
json-formatter.seoTitle: JSON Formatter and Validator — Free, Private, No Upload | FileFolks
json-formatter.seoDescription: Format, validate, and minify JSON online. Runs entirely in your browser. Your data never leaves your device.

faqs.question: Is my JSON data sent to a server?
faqs.answer: No. All processing happens in your browser using JavaScript. Your data never leaves your device. You can verify this by opening your browser's Network tab while using the tool.

faqs.question: What is the maximum JSON size I can format?
faqs.answer: There is no hard limit, but very large files (over 50 MB) may cause your browser tab to slow down. For best performance, we recommend files under 10 MB.

faqs.question: Can I format JSON with comments (JSONC)?
faqs.answer: Standard JSON does not support comments. If your input contains comments, the validator will report a parse error. You can strip comments manually before formatting.

faqs.question: What is the difference between formatting and minifying?
faqs.answer: Formatting adds indentation and line breaks to make JSON human-readable. Minifying removes all unnecessary whitespace to reduce file size, which is useful when JSON will be sent over a network.

Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.metaDescription: FileFolks processes all files locally in your browser. No data is ever uploaded or stored.
